relabelled_senses.definition: Practical and realistic in attitude or approach; not pretentious or fanciful.
To stop work as a deliberate action, typically as part of a strike or protest.
To be engaged in the final, detailed stages of a project or task, focusing on small, precise actions needed to complete it.
Until the last possible moment; with the outcome undecided until the very end.
To delay or be deliberately slow in performing an action, especially out of reluctance, resistance, or lack of urgency.
To urinate (used as a slang euphemism, typically by or about a man).
To urinate (a slang expression, commonly used by or referring to a man).
A person (typically male) who habitually reacts to situations with exaggerated emotion or theatrics, often to attract attention or cause disruption.
A person (often female, but used for any gender) who consistently responds to events with excessive emotion, exaggerated behavior, or attention-seeking theatrics.
To be unable to remember something; to fail to recall a particular fact, name, or detail at a given moment.
To exaggerate or stretch the truth; to relate a story or claim that is implausible or unlikely.
To live or behave in a straightforward, honest, and upright manner; to follow a steady, morally correct course.
To reach equality or parity, as in score, position, or quantity; to become level with another party.
To attract or provoke enemy gunfire or other hostile weapons fire toward a person, unit, or object, often to protect others or to locate enemy positions.
A person, feature, or attraction that draws people to an event, place, or business; a main attraction or crowd-puller.
To take one's final breath; to die.
To end play in a cricket match by removing the stumps; to stop play or finish for the day.
To set a limit beyond which one will not go or will not tolerate certain behaviour or actions.
To exaggerate, embellish, or tell an implausible or highly unlikely story; to stretch the truth for dramatic effect.
To be chosen, by chance or selection, for the least desirable task, duty, or outcome; to suffer an unlucky fate relative to others.
